summary: Tested blockchain to verify certificates of origin for goods imported under NAFTA and CAFTA trade agreements, replacing paper-based processes and reducing redundant data submission across supply chain parties.
Enabled secure, interoperable data exchange between CBP, manufacturers, retailers, and rights holders to detect counterfeit goods on imports using W3C Verifiable Credentials and DIDs.

project: Supply Chain Traceability — Steel, E-Commerce, Food, Oil & Gas
summary: Funded Transmute, mesur.io, and Neoflow/Mavennet to build interoperable verifiable credential systems for cross-border supply chain data exchange with CBP. Successfully completed real-time data exchange testing in October 2023.

project: FASt Lane — IT Schedule 70 Blockchain Procurement
summary: Applied blockchain to the Multiple Award Schedules proposal review process, creating a transparent audit trail and automating financial reviews. Reduced contract award timelines from approximately 100 days to under 10 days.

agency: Treasury / Bureau of the Fiscal Service
summary: Tested whether blockchain could reduce financial reporting and compliance burden on federal grant recipients by providing near real-time visibility into grant fund transfers across agency boundaries (Treasury, NSF, GAO). Final JFMIP report published December 2023.

project: Blockchain Analytics for Cryptocurrency Tax Enforcement
summary: IRS-CI uses Chainalysis blockchain analytics tools to trace crypto transactions, identify tax evasion, and seize illicitly held digital assets. IRS-CI has seized an estimated $10 billion in cryptocurrency.

project: SIMBA Chain — Supply Chain & Budget Tokenization (STRATFI)
summary: Blockchain-based applications for USAF supply chain management, parts tracking, and budget tokenization. $30M STRATFI award scaling across Navy, Army, and Defense Logistics Agency.
First live use of blockchain in a U.S. Army operational environment. Tracked 600 tons of equipment for supply chain auditability during Army Materiel Command operations.

project: DSCSA Drug Supply Chain Blockchain Pilot
summary: Tested blockchain-based interoperability for tracking and tracing prescription drugs through the pharmaceutical supply chain under the Drug Supply Chain Security Act, involving 22+ organizations.

project: IBM Blockchain POC for Food Export Certification
summary: Evaluated whether DLT could add immutability, visibility, and efficiency to multi-country documentary requirements of the food export certification process.

Internal discussions in early 2025 about using blockchain to monitor federal housing grants and potentially paying grant recipients in stablecoins. Not implemented; officially denied by HUD spokesperson.

project: Veterans Affairs Distributed Ledger Innovation Act (H.R. 3455)
summary: Would require VA to study how blockchain could improve traceability, fraud resistance, and transparency in veterans benefits adjudication. No actual pilot launched yet.

Utah County began issuing digital marriage licenses as verifiable credentials on blockchain, making them instantly verifiable and tamper-evident. One of the first government-issued vital records on a distributed ledger in the U.S.

project: Citizen-Centered Federal DLT — Self-Sovereign Identity for Government Services
summary: A proposed framework where citizens own their digital identities through self-sovereign identity systems, selectively sharing data with agencies rather than agencies controlling personal information. Envisions phased rollout from federal integration to inter-agency coordination to citizen participation.

project: Decentralized Identifier (DID) Verification Across Two Agencies
summary: Demonstrate how a citizen who has established a DID can present it to multiple federal agencies for verification — e.g., proving an address — without each agency maintaining its own identity store. References USAID, NIST, DHS, and TSA digital identity work.
